title: Co-injection of  α-synuclein PFFs and retrovirus for retro labeling in mouse striatum
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e1v9","text":"This protocol describes the procedure to co-inject alpha-synuclein preformed fibrils with retro GFP-AAV into the striatum for tracing of pathology. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Dilute recombinant mouse α-synuclein PFFs to 2 mg/ml with sterile PBS Only use high quality PBS suitable for in vivo work.
Anesthetize mouse with 2-3% isoflurane with an isoflurane vaporizer
Sonicate PFFs as indicated in the protocol ( https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.14egn69qql5d/v1 )
Add AAV Retrograde virus, AAVrg/GFP, to sonicated PFFs to a final titer of 8x10 8 GC/μl

Once anesthetized ( i.e. , unresponsive to pain and with normal breathing rate), transfer mouseto the stereotaxic frame fitted with a heating pad set to 37°C Position the nose cone for isoflurane delivery correctly to maintain the level of anesthesia
Apply eye lubricant to both eyes to prevent corneal damage
Locate the dorsal striatal injection site relative to Bregma: AP +1.0 mm, ML +1.5 mm, DV+3.0 mm from the dura mater.
Make an incision in the skin above the skull , and clean the area with a dry cotton swab.
Pain Management: Inject 30 μl of local anesthetic ( lidocaine, bupivacaine ) subcutaneously along the incision site. Administer preoperative Ethiqa XR 3.25 mg/kg.
Remove the cream and clean the area with 70% ethanol .
Remove remaining fur by applying depilatory cream for one minute . Depilatory cream can burn the skin if applied longer than 1 minute.
Shave fur above the skull with a hair trimmer.

Inject 2.5 μl of the PFF with AAVrg/GFP mixture (5 μg of PFF and 2× 10 9 GC total), with a rate of 0.35 μl/min with an injector pump. Our injection setup is a Pump 11 Elite (Harvard Apparatus) with a 10 μl Hamilton syringe (Hamilton, 1701RN) connected to a medical-grade tubing (Tygon, AAD04091) equipped with a 30G needle.
Drill a hole at the desired coordinates without causing damage to the brain . Be careful not to break the dura to prevent excessive bleeding.
Wait 5 minutes after the full volume is injected. Then slowly withdraw the needle injection before removing the needle.
Close the incision with absorbable sutures, and administer Carprofen (5 mg/kg,Subcutaneous)
creator: Maria Matos
